トピック.授業計画1:
<Lecture> Each topic will be introduced on a three-week cycle following a problem-solution genre. The first week will introduce an SDG, providing background information and important definitions. The second week will address the causes of the problem and the interrelatedness of the topic with other SDGs. The third week will introduce a solution to the problem and discuss its successes and limitations. 

<Discussion> Materials and topics will follow the lecture course while also introducing discussion skills and improving vocabulary knowledge. 

<Writing> The majority of the course will be spent researching, analyzing sources and paragraphs, and writing academic essays. 

<Special Topics> The schedule for each Special Topics course will depend on the teacher and will be announced to students at the beginning of the class.
トピック.授業外学習2:
<Lecture> Students will read and complete preparation worksheets for each lecture. Students will need to review materials for a midterm and final exam. 

<Discussion> To prepare for the discussions, students will read articles, attend the lectures, and research the topic on the Internet. In the discussions, they present, discuss, and explore the topics based on the lecture, reading, and research. 

<Writing> Students research sources and write academic essays as well as prepare for a quiz. 

<Special Topics> Each Special Topics course will have different homework associated with the topic.

教科書/Required texts0: Materials will be available on LUNA. You should bring your materials to class on your device (computer / tablet). Some special topics courses may require other materials. Your teacher will let you know on the first day of class if you need to purchase anything extra.
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each subject is placed on the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school.By studying these subjects with the DP and CP in mind, you will develop the abilities expected by each faculty and graduate school.The DP, CP and curriculum charts (list of subjects, etc.) for each faculty and graduate school are posted in the link below, so please use this as reference for your study plan.https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1*This is a link to kwic (only current students can view this)
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note <Lecture> The grade will be based on three tests: unit 1 test, unit 2 test, and the final exam. There are also pop quizzes. This means they are not announced before the class. If a student is absent, these quizzes cannot be made up. Instead, each student's lowest quiz grade will be dropped so that students can miss one quiz without it affecting their final grade. There is also one homework submission. <Discussion> Grades are based on spoken performance in 3-4 person discussions, including the expression of required contents in English and interactive response skills. In addition, presentation and minor speaking tasks are required.<Writing> Assessment includes folio writing, outlining, draft writing and completing a quiz.  <Special Topics> Each Special Topics Course is graded differently. Your teacher will provide you with the course syllabus on the first day of class.

name: 【200】英語コミュニケーションV　Ｃ／English Communication 5
担当者: ジョーダン　Ｊ．（JORDAN JENNIFER ALINE）
成績評価備考:
備考　Note
<Lecture> The grade will be based on three tests: unit 1 test, unit 2 test, and the final exam. There are also pop quizzes. This means they are not announced before the class. If a student is absent, these quizzes cannot be made up. Instead, each student's lowest quiz grade will be dropped so that students can miss one quiz without it affecting their final grade. There is also one homework submission. 
<Discussion> Grades are based on spoken performance in 3-4 person discussions, including the expression of required contents in English and interactive response skills. In addition, presentation and minor speaking tasks are required.
<Writing> Assessment includes folio writing, outlining, draft writing and completing a quiz.  
<Special Topics> Each Special Topics Course is graded differently. Your teacher will provide you with the course syllabus on the first day of class.
